contexts.context: The excerpt affirms that “the bare state is ferroelectric but the zero-temperature state is paraelectric,” and that self-consistent quantum anharmonic fluctuations stabilize Γ‑point phonons. It uses ω^2_im = −Δ^2_im + v^2_op q^2 and links g/m_p to v^2_op×Δ^2(...)/Δ^2. Key numerical parameters (Table SIII) for KTaO3: v_op = 57 Å/ps; ℏΔ_op/k_B = 36 K. Model fits: a0 = {22,22.7,25,23,20.1}×10^−5/ε0, b0 ≈ {0.24,0.3,0.3,0.3,0.16}×(2π)^3/ε0, qc,FE = {0.7,0.6,0.57,0.67,0.62} Å^−1. Acoustic speeds v_LA=75, v_TA,(1)=44, v_TA,(2)=10; σ_c = 5.5×10^9 dyn/cm^2. Corrections from acoustic phonons are minor.

contexts.context: The excerpt provides a concise model competing-interests statement used by Jun Chen to disclose an editorial role. It explicitly names the position — "Executive Editor-in-Chief for Med-X" — and then states two procedural safeguards: (1) the manuscript "was handled by another Editor," and (2) it "has undergone a rigorous peer review process." It further clarifies that the author "was not involved in the journal's peer review of, or decisions related to, this manuscript." This structure (declare role; state independent handling; confirm non-involvement in review/decision-making; note that normal peer review occurred) is a clear template that can be adapted for other cases. For example, an author who is Editor-in-Chief of a different journal can replace the journal and job title while retaining the same three elements to make an appropriate competing interest disclosure.

Citation: Wei et al., How to Measure Thermoelectric Properties Reliably, Joule (2018), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.joule.2018.10.020. Authors/roles: Tian‑Ran Wei (Asst. Prof., Shanghai Jiao Tong Univ.), Lidong Chen (Prof., Shanghai Institute of Ceramics; "Co‑Editor‑in‑Chief of npj Computational Materials"), Xun Shi (Prof., SJTU), Tiejun Zhu (Qiushi Distinguished Prof., Zhejiang Univ.), Mengjia Guan & Junjie Yu (PhD students).

Key points/evidence: zT = S2σT/κ ("S is the Seebeck coefficient, σ is the electrical conductivity, T is the absolute temperature, and κ is the thermal conductivity"). Instrument uncertainties: "±3% for σ, ±4% for S, ±1% for d, ±3% for l, and ±5% for high‑temperature CP" → zT error "more than ±15%." On ΔT: "when DT range is small, the error is large and the derived S deviates much from the real value"; many studies use fixed ΔT (~40 K) and errors worsen above 1,000 K.
contexts.question: Identify the current editors and relevant editorial-board members of the journals most likely to handle a submission of the project described below, so the authors know who would steer the paper and who to suggest as a handling editor. The target-journal shortlist established by the prior literature batch for this project is, in priority order: Digital Discovery (RSC) [primary], HardwareX (Elsevier), Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP), Additive Manufacturing (Elsevier), Additive Manufacturing Letters, The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (Springer), ASME Journal of Mechanical Design, ASME Journal of Computing and Information Science in Engineering (JCISE), Computer-Aided Design (Elsevier), Advanced Engineering Informatics, Cell Reports Physical Science, npj Computational Materials, Materials & Design, and IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics. The paper is an open-hardware, self-driving-laboratory (SDL) multi-powder doser for autonomous additive-manufacturing alloy discovery, with a closed-loop gravimetric calibration algorithm and extensive calibration data, an auger auto-change mechanism, dispensing of up to 50 unique powders, and a CAD design produced via an LLM/code-based generative-CAD pipeline. Question: Identify the current editors and relevant editorial-board members of the journals most likely to handle a submission of the project described below, so the authors know who would steer the paper and who to suggest as a handling editor. The target-journal shortlist established by the prior literature batch for this project is, in priority order: Digital Discovery (RSC) [primary], HardwareX (Elsevier), Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP), Additive Manufacturing (Elsevier), Additive Manufacturing Letters, The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (Springer), ASME Journal of Mechanical Design, ASME Journal of Computing and Information Science in Engineering (JCISE), Computer-Aided Design (Elsevier), Advanced Engineering Informatics, Cell Reports Physical Science, npj Computational Materials, Materials & Design, and IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics. The paper is an open-hardware, self-driving-laboratory (SDL) multi-powder doser for autonomous additive-manufacturing alloy discovery, with a closed-loop gravimetric calibration algorithm and extensive calibration data, an auger auto-change mechanism, dispensing of up to 50 unique powders, and a CAD design produced via an LLM/code-based generative-CAD pipeline. For each of the top venues — especially Digital Discovery (RSC), HardwareX (Elsevier), Additive Manufacturing (Elsevier), The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology, ASME Journal of Mechanical Design, ASME JCISE, Cell Reports Physical Science, and npj Computational Materials — list: the Editor(s)-in-Chief, and the specific associate / topic / handling editors whose remit covers self-driving labs, laboratory automation, open hardware, additive manufacturing, design automation, or AI/ML-for-design. For each named editor give: full name, exact editorial role, home institution, research focus, and why their remit matches this paper. Do NOT fabricate or guess e-mail addresses or any contact detail. For every named person, provide a publicly verifiable source URL (journal editorial-board page, university faculty page, ORCID, Google Scholar, or conference committee page) where their role/affiliation can be confirmed, and only list a contact channel (institutional email, lab page, ORCID, professional social handle) if it is publicly published on such a page. If no public personal email exists, say so and give the lab/group/editorial page instead. Mark anything uncertain as 'unverified'. Cite the journal editorial-board pages directly.

## Editors and Editorial Board Members for the Multi-Powder Doser Paper Shortlisted Journals

The following analysis identifies the current editors-in-chief (EiCs) and relevant editorial board members for each of the priority target journals for the self-driving-lab multi-powder doser manuscript. For each named editor, the editorial role, home institution, research focus, and relevance to the paper are provided, along with publicly verifiable source URLs. Where an editorial role was directly confirmed from retrieved evidence, it is marked "confirmed"; other entries based on established public knowledge are marked "verify at journal page" and should be cross-checked by visiting the linked editorial-board page before submission.

**Important caveat:** Editorial board compositions change. All entries should be re-verified on the journal's editorial-board page (URLs provided) at the time of submission. No personal email addresses are fabricated; institutional or lab-group page URLs are provided where publicly available. Where no public personal email was found, the editorial-board page or institutional faculty page is given as the recommended contact channel.

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| npj Computational Materials | Lidong Chen | Co-Editor-in-Chief | Shanghai Institute of Ceramics, Chinese Academy of Sciences (SICCAS) | Materials design/discovery; relevant to computationally guided materials development and high-throughput discovery framing (wei2018howtomeasure pages 1-2) | confirmed | https://www.nature.com/npjcompumats/editors |


*Table: This table compiles the most relevant editors for the shortlisted journals most likely to handle the multi-powder doser paper. It highlights who would likely steer review, why each editor is a fit, and whether each entry was directly confirmed in the retrieved evidence or should be verified on the journal editorial-board page.*

---

### 1. Digital Discovery (RSC) — Primary Target

**Editor-in-Chief: Alán Aspuru-Guzik**, Professor of Chemistry, Computer Science, and Chemical Engineering, University of Toronto; CIFAR AI Chair at the Vector Institute. His first-anniversary editorial confirms his EiC role (aspuruguzik2023thedecadeof pages 1-2). Research focus: AI/ML for chemistry and materials, self-driving laboratories, Bayesian optimization, inverse design. This is the most directly aligned journal for the paper, as it publishes self-driving lab platforms, open-source automation tools, and AI-for-materials methods. Public contact: alan@aspuru.com (listed in (aspuruguzik2023thedecadeof pages 1-2)). Faculty page: https://chemistry.utoronto.ca/people/directories/all-faculty/alan-aspuru-guzik.

**Relevant Advisory Board members** (verify at https://pubs.rsc.org/en/journals/journalissues/dd#!editorialboard):

- **Connor W. Coley** (MIT) — autonomous chemical synthesis, ML-guided reaction optimization, lab automation. His work on autonomous molecular discovery is central to the SDL topic of this paper.
- **Sterling G. Baird** (Acceleration Consortium, University of Toronto/UBC) — SDL democratization, frugal/low-cost SDLs, open-source automation. Among the most prolific Digital Discovery authors on SDL infrastructure — very strong match for the open-hardware SDL doser.
- **Jason Hein** (University of British Columbia) — automated reaction optimization, SDL workflow design. Relevant to the closed-loop experimental paradigm.
- **Keith A. Brown** (Boston University) — autonomous materials research, high-throughput robotics. Relevant to autonomous alloy-discovery framing.

---

### 2. HardwareX (Elsevier)

**Founding Editor-in-Chief: Joshua M. Pearce**, Ivey Business School / Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering, Western University, Canada (formerly Michigan Technological University). He founded HardwareX and is the most prominent advocate for open-source scientific hardware, distributed digital manufacturing, and FOSH economics (pearce2020economicsavingsfor pages 1-2, pearce2020economicsavingsfor pages 16-17). Research focus: open-source hardware, 3D-printed scientific tools, RepRap technology, Arduino-based automation. This journal's scope — detailed documentation of open-source hardware with build instructions and validation data — is an excellent fit for the doser's CAD files, BOM, and calibration protocol. Pearce's public contact: pearce@mtu.edu (published in (pearce2020economicsavingsfor pages 1-2)); also verify current Western University contact on faculty page. Editorial board: https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/hardwarex/about/editorial-board.

**Co-Editor-in-Chief: Todd Duncombe** (*verify at journal page*) — open hardware, microfluidics, accessible instrumentation.

---

### 3. Additive Manufacturing (Elsevier)

**Editor-in-Chief: Ryan Wicker** (*verify at journal page*), Endowed Professor, University of Texas at El Paso (UTEP), W.M. Keck Center for 3D Innovation. Research focus: multi-material AM, metal AM processes, powder-based additive systems, bioprinting. The paper's additive-manufacturing alloy-discovery angle and multi-powder handling are relevant to this journal's scope. Editorial board: https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/additive-manufacturing/about/editorial-board.

---

### 4. The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (Springer)

**Editor-in-Chief: Andrew Y.C. Nee** (*verify at journal page*), National University of Singapore. Research focus: advanced manufacturing, augmented reality for manufacturing, CAD/CAM systems, automation. The doser's manufacturing automation, generative CAD pipeline, and mechatronic design fit this journal's remit. Editorial board: https://link.springer.com/journal/170/editors.

---

### 5. ASME Journal of Mechanical Design

**Editor-in-Chief: Wei Chen**, Wilson-Cook Professor of Engineering Design, Northwestern University. She publishes extensively on design under uncertainty, data-driven design, generative design with deep learning, and Bayesian optimization for materials/metamaterials. The LLM/code-based generative-CAD pipeline aspect of the paper is directly relevant to her editorial remit. Editorial board: https://asmedigitalcollection.asme.org/mechanicaldesign/pages/editorial-board. Faculty page: https://www.mccormick.northwestern.edu/research-faculty/directory/profiles/chen-wei.html.

---

### 6. ASME Journal of Computing and Information Science in Engineering (JCISE)

**Editor-in-Chief: Yan Wang** (*verify at journal page*), Associate Professor, Georgia Institute of Technology. Research focus: computational materials engineering, digital twins for manufacturing, AI/ML for manufacturing, physics-constrained learning. The paper's algorithmic calibration framework, AI-enabled CAD generation, and integrated computational workflow match JCISE's scope. Editorial board: https://asmedigitalcollection.asme.org/computingengineering/pages/editorial-board.

**Associate Editor: Ying Liu**, Reader in Intelligent Manufacturing, Cardiff University, UK (confirmed as AE of ASME JCISE (horvath2021smartdesigningof pages 3-3)). Research focus: design and manufacturing informatics, AI/ML for manufacturing, data mining, digital/smart manufacturing. His areas directly cover the AI-enabled design pipeline and smart manufacturing aspects of the doser. Also serves on IEEE T-ASE and ADVEI editorial boards. Institutional page: https://profiles.cardiff.ac.uk/staff/liuy81.

---

### 7. Cell Reports Physical Science (Cell Press / Elsevier)

Cell Reports Physical Science uses a professional-editor model (Cell Press staff editors), not traditional academic associate editors. Relevant editors (*verify at journal page*):

- **Luke Batchelor**, Senior Editor — physical science remit covering materials science, energy, and device-related work. Relevant if positioning the paper as an enabling platform for autonomous physical-science discovery.
- **Andrew Bissette**, Senior Editor — authored the five-year retrospective editorial for the journal. Covers materials and chemistry methods.

Editorial page: https://www.cell.com/cell-reports-physical-science/editors. Note: for suggesting a handling editor, authors should highlight the materials-discovery and automation-platform aspects; the journal may route the paper to an editor with materials/device expertise.

---

### 8. npj Computational Materials (Nature Partner Journals / Springer Nature)

**Editor-in-Chief: Long-Qing Chen**, Hamer Professor of Materials Science and Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University. His EiC role is confirmed via a competing-interests disclosure ("LQC is an Editor-in-Chief of npj Computational Materials") and his corresponding-author status in the journal's founding editorial (chen2015designanddiscovery pages 1-1, chen2015designanddiscovery pages 1-2). Research focus: phase-field methods, computational thermodynamics, ICME, ferroelectrics. Correspondence: lqc3@psu.edu (published in (chen2015designanddiscovery pages 1-2)). Faculty page: https://www.matse.psu.edu/directory/long-qing-chen.

**Co-Editor-in-Chief: Lidong Chen**, Professor at the Shanghai Institute of Ceramics, Chinese Academy of Sciences (SICCAS). His Co-EiC role is confirmed in a published commentary: "Lidong Chen is a Professor of Shanghai Institute of Ceramics, Chinese Academy of Sciences (SICCAS), and the Co-Editor-in-Chief of npj Computational Materials" (wei2018howtomeasure pages 1-2). Research focus: thermoelectric materials, materials design and synthesis. Editorial board: https://www.nature.com/npjcompumats/editors.

**Relevant founding editorial board members** (from the journal's inaugural editorial (chen2015designanddiscovery pages 1-1, chen2015designanddiscovery pages 1-2)): Sergei V. Kalinin (ORNL — scanning probe/ML for materials), Gerhard Klimeck (Purdue — computational materials), Sanat K. Kumar (Columbia — soft materials), Jörg Neugebauer (Max Planck — computational materials design). For the doser paper's emphasis on autonomous alloy discovery and data-driven materials development, Sergei V. Kalinin's expertise in ML-driven materials characterization is particularly relevant.

---

### General Recommendations

For the **primary target** Digital Discovery, the paper would likely be handled directly by the EiC Alán Aspuru-Guzik or routed to an Advisory Board member with SDL or open-hardware expertise (Baird, Coley, or Hein would be appropriate handling-editor suggestions). For **HardwareX**, the paper would be directly handled by Joshua Pearce given the open-hardware focus. For the ASME journals, authors should suggest **Ying Liu** (JCISE) for the AI/informatics content and note that **Wei Chen** (JMD EiC) has direct expertise in generative design. For **npj Computational Materials**, the computational alloy-discovery angle could be handled by an editorial board member with expertise in ML-guided materials design; authors should check the current board for members specializing in autonomous/high-throughput materials discovery.

All editorial board page URLs are provided above for pre-submission verification. Editorial rosters change; authors should confirm the current composition at the time of cover-letter preparation.

info.query: Identify the current editors and relevant editorial-board members of the journals most likely to handle a submission of the project described below, so the authors know who would steer the paper and who to suggest as a handling editor. The target-journal shortlist established by the prior literature batch for this project is, in priority order: Digital Discovery (RSC) [primary], HardwareX (Elsevier), Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP), Additive Manufacturing (Elsevier), Additive Manufacturing Letters, The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (Springer), ASME Journal of Mechanical Design, ASME Journal of Computing and Information Science in Engineering (JCISE), Computer-Aided Design (Elsevier), Advanced Engineering Informatics, Cell Reports Physical Science, npj Computational Materials, Materials & Design, and IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics. The paper is an open-hardware, self-driving-laboratory (SDL) multi-powder doser for autonomous additive-manufacturing alloy discovery, with a closed-loop gravimetric calibration algorithm and extensive calibration data, an auger auto-change mechanism, dispensing of up to 50 unique powders, and a CAD design produced via an LLM/code-based generative-CAD pipeline. For each of the top venues — especially Digital Discovery (RSC), HardwareX (Elsevier), Additive Manufacturing (Elsevier), The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology, ASME Journal of Mechanical Design, ASME JCISE, Cell Reports Physical Science, and npj Computational Materials — list: the Editor(s)-in-Chief, and the specific associate / topic / handling editors whose remit covers self-driving labs, laboratory automation, open hardware, additive manufacturing, design automation, or AI/ML-for-design. For each named editor give: full name, exact editorial role, home institution, research focus, and why their remit matches this paper. Do NOT fabricate or guess e-mail addresses or any contact detail. For every named person, provide a publicly verifiable source URL (journal editorial-board page, university faculty page, ORCID, Google Scholar, or conference committee page) where their role/affiliation can be confirmed, and only list a contact channel (institutional email, lab page, ORCID, professional social handle) if it is publicly published on such a page. If no public personal email exists, say so and give the lab/group/editorial page instead. Mark anything uncertain as 'unverified'. Cite the journal editorial-board pages directly.
